chapters.title: — Round 2: Schenone (IUT for Tumor-Related Fetal Anemia)
— Round 4: Yun (HOMA-IR and PCOS Fertility Outcomes)
— Close: What to Watch / Still Unsettled

follow_up_links.reason: Largest post-licensure safety study of RSVpreF in pregnancy; no signal for PTB, stillbirth, SGA, or HDP at recommended gestational window
follow_up_links.title: Association of Respiratory Syncytial Virus Vaccination During Pregnancy With Adverse Obstetric and Neonatal Outcomes

follow_up_links.reason: First RCT comparing balloon tamponade durations; may shorten standard dwell time if noninferiority confirmed
follow_up_links.title: Intrauterine Balloon Tamponade Duration for Postpartum Hemorrhage: A Randomized Controlled Trial

follow_up_links.reason: Aggregates rare-condition outcome data for IUT in tumor-related fetal anemia; useful for counseling despite limited case numbers
follow_up_links.title: Perinatal outcomes following intrauterine transfusion for fetal anemia secondary to fetal or placental tumor: systematic review

follow_up_links.reason: Conceptual framework for preeclampsia phenotyping; may shape future research and guideline development
follow_up_links.title: Pre-Eclampsia Beyond the Binary: Rethinking Phenotypes for Clinical Use

follow_up_links.reason: HOMA-IR as preconception predictor of fertility outcomes in PCOS; actionable for risk stratification
follow_up_links.title: Prepregnancy Insulin Resistance and Fertility and Pregnancy Outcomes in Women With Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome

key_claims.claim: RSVpreF vaccination at 32–36 weeks gestation is not associated with increased preterm birth, stillbirth, small-for-gestational-age, or hypertensive disorders of pregnancy
Six-hour intrauterine balloon tamponade duration may be noninferior to eighteen-hour duration for postpartum hemorrhage after vaginal delivery
Intrauterine transfusion for fetal anemia from fetal or placental tumors has variable perinatal outcomes based on limited case series data
Preeclampsia may be better understood through phenotype-based classification rather than binary categorization
Higher prepregnancy insulin resistance independently predicts lower ovulation, clinical pregnancy, and live-birth rates in women with PCOS
